title: Add a parachute
description: Upgrade your rocket with a nylon parachute for gentle landings and possible reuse.

dialogue.text: Your first launch went great. Let's add a parachute so the rocket returns in one piece and can fly again.

options.text: Sounds like a plan! How do we go about adding a parachute?

dialogue.text: A parachute slows descent so the rocket survives landing. Gather a launch-capable model rocket, parachute, kevlar shock cord, parachute harness kit and flame-resistant recovery wadding. The wadding shields the parachute from the motor's ejection charge. Wear eye protection and keep the motor removed while assembling.

options.text: Great! Once the parachute system is ready, are we good to go for another launch?

dialogue.text: With the parachute fitted, set the rocket on a model rocket launchpad. Insert a rocket igniter, hook it to the launch controller and keep everyone at least 5 m away. Follow the rocket launch checklist, then press the button and watch it fly!

options.text: Congratulations on another successful launch! How did the rocket fare with the parachute?

dialogue.text: The parachute deployed and the rocket touched down softly. Nice work upgrading the design!

options.text: Thank you, Nova! I'm thrilled with the progress we've made!
